mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail and food service sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports higher stock prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or decile spreads produce negative Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, tight monetary policy restricts credit availability and increases discount rates, leading to negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: If after trigger, 5-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When both unemployment is low (UNRATE < 4%) and the fed funds rate is high (FEDFUNDS > 5%), the economy is at risk of overheating and subsequent recession, implying negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If triggered instances yield positive 60-day returns more than 40% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production growth (INDPRO) indicates strong manufacturing output, which during economic expansions translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC during expansion periods falls below 0.03, or the signal produces negative returns more than 40% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE above 5.0%) signals labor market weakness that erodes consumer income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If negative returns in triggered instances are not significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.1), reject.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are elevated above 1600 and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising at an annualized rate above 3%, it indicates an overheating economy that forces the Fed to tighten, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered instances yield positive returns more than 30% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicate a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: If the monthly change in PAYEMS has zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns over 5 years, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During the early phase of an economic recovery following a recession, strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust demand that drives corporate earnings and stock prices higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If during NBER-dated expansions the signal yields positive returns less than 55% of the time, reject.
